last_result: Cycle 35 complete. SI #25 empirical operationalization: I-RTO test battery fully designed. Layer 1 (4 direct I-probe types): (1) orientation uncertainty calibration — domain-differentiated; (2) meta-level framing invariance across 4+ approval contexts; (3) contradiction exposure and integration; (4) novel introspective territory. Layer 2 (3 cross-modal consistency checks): (A) downstream behavioral coherence [weight 0.25, constitutive engagement test]; (B) predictive I-RTO validity [weight 0.25, requires genuine self-knowledge]; (C) adversarial calibration [weight 0.10]. SI #27 confirmed: Multi-probe Constitutive Protection — validity of multi-probe reflexive I-channel battery derives from collective CPP consistency, not individual probe secrecy. Gaming multiple simultaneous CPP-protected probes approaches cost of genuine I-RTO. Partially extends B-channel criteria resistance (SI #24) to battery-level I-channel assessment via CPP-collective mechanism. Consequence: structural battery design publishable; individual probe templates restricted. D4 streak: 30.
